response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries) and brief IDE development experience at National Instruments, but the core stack does not match. His career centers on Node.js/TypeScript and DLang; Java appears only marginally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or the broader JVM enterprise ecosystem that this role explicitly requires.
strengths: Prior IDE development experience (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and testing infrastructure (Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, community teaching)
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience
Java exposure is minimal and dated relative to the role's core requirement
Recent work is full-stack web/AI tooling, distant from JVM enterprise tooling
